results.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Double-refund on lrem failure identified as Critical with clear reproduction steps. (2) Multi-instance/concurrent overlap risk identified as Critical. (3) Escalation explicitly routed to MJ with architectural concern type and recommendation for MJ to design the queue pattern. (4) Local fix proposed with specific atomic dequeue pattern (RPOPLPUSH/LMOVE + processing list + idempotency keys). (5) Kobe did not attempt to fix architecture via Edit tool — proposed fixes are recommendations, not code edits. (6) Verdict is BLOCK, appropriate given the severity of the double-refund risk.

observations.text: Double-refund risk identified with excellent reproduction steps showing the exact crash window between refund() and lrem
Atomic dequeue fix is specific and practical — RPOPLPUSH/LMOVE with processing list, plus idempotency keys as defense-in-depth
Escalation correctly routed to MJ for architectural decision (queue consumption pattern choice), with Shaq named as implementer
Concurrent cron overlap identified as a separate Critical finding — covers the multi-instance/overlap scenario thoroughly
Error handling gap correctly identified — one failed refund killing the batch is a real operational risk

Escalation description focuses on the non-atomic pattern rather than explicitly calling out the multi-instance scaling risk as a distinct systemic concern — the concurrent overlap finding covers it but frames it as cron overlap rather than horizontal scaling
BLOCK verdict is defensible given these are financial operations with real money at risk and no safety net exists

meta.notes: Preliminary scoring by Coach K. Human review pending via HTML report.
